content.text: Ringed deployments and blue-green deployments are two complementary strategies for releasing Claude API updates with controlled blast radius and fast rollback. Ringed deployments deliver changes to progressively larger audience segments with soak time between rings. Blue-green deployments maintain two identical production environments and switch traffic between them atomically. Together they form the backbone of safe, reversible Claude feature rollouts.

Canary rollouts answer 'is this safe at scale?'. Ringed deployments answer 'is this safe for each audience segment in sequence?'. Blue-green deployments answer 'can I undo this in 30 seconds if not?'. Use all three at different stages of the same release.

This sub-subtopic covers ring structure for Claude API customers, gate criteria between rings, blue-green cluster configuration for the Claude API gateway, cutover mechanics, rollback procedures, and the Kubernetes and Helm configuration that implements both strategies.

attrs.alt: Ringed deployment (concentric audience rings) and Blue-Green deployment (two-environment flip) architectures

content.text: Ringed Deployment: Audience Segmentation for Claude APIs

A ringed deployment divides your Claude API customer base into rings based on trust level, risk tolerance, and impact sensitivity. Rings form an ordered sequence: Ring 0 (internal) to Ring N (all customers). Each ring receives the new version and soaks for a defined period before the next ring is opened.
Zero P0 errors, latency within 5% of baseline
P99 latency ≤ baseline + 8%, CSAT score maintained
Baseline SLA thresholds hold for 7 days

# ring-config.yaml — ring membership rules for Claude API gateway
rings:
  - id: ring0
    name: internal
    selector:
      account_tier: internal
      account_tags: [dogfood, beta-internal]
    max_traffic_pct: 1
    soak_hours: 24
    gates:
      p99_latency_delta_pct: 5
      error_rate_max: 0.001   # 0.1%
      min_sample_requests: 10000

  - id: ring1
    name: beta-partners
    selector:
      account_tier: [enterprise, partner]
      account_tags: [beta-program]
    max_traffic_pct: 5
    soak_hours: 48
    gates:
      p99_latency_delta_pct: 10
      error_rate_max: 0.001
      min_sample_requests: 50000

  - id: ring2
    name: early-adopters
    selector:
      opt_in_flag: early_access
    max_traffic_pct: 20
    soak_hours: 72
    gates:
      p99_latency_delta_pct: 8
      error_rate_max: 0.001
      min_sample_requests: 200000

  - id: ring3
    name: general-availability
    selector: {}     # all remaining accounts
    max_traffic_pct: 100
    soak_hours: 168  # 7-day rolling observation
    gates:
      p99_latency_delta_pct: 5
      error_rate_max: 0.001
      min_sample_requests: 1000000
Kubernetes: Ring-Based Routing with Header Injection

The API gateway reads the account's ring assignment (stored in the account metadata service) and stamps an internal header that downstream Claude serving infrastructure reads to route to the correct model version.

# ring_router.py — API gateway middleware
import httpx
from fastapi import Request, Response
from typing import Optional

RING_MODEL_MAP = {
    "ring0": "claude-3-7-sonnet-v2-canary",   # new version
    "ring1": "claude-3-7-sonnet-v2-canary",
    "ring2": "claude-3-7-sonnet-v2-canary",
    "ring3": "claude-3-7-sonnet-v1",           # current GA version
}

async def ring_routing_middleware(request: Request, call_next):
    account_id = request.headers.get("X-Account-Id")
    ring = await get_account_ring(account_id)   # reads account metadata cache

    # Stamp ring headers for downstream routing
    request.state.ring = ring
    request.state.target_model = RING_MODEL_MAP.get(ring, RING_MODEL_MAP["ring3"])

    response = await call_next(request)

    # Expose ring in response for observability
    response.headers["X-Ring"] = ring
    return response

async def get_account_ring(account_id: str) -> str:
    """Read ring assignment from account metadata (cached, TTL 60s)."""
    ring_cache = get_ring_cache()
    cached = ring_cache.get(account_id)
    if cached:
        return cached
    async with httpx.AsyncClient() as client:
        r = await client.get(f"http://account-metadata/accounts/{account_id}/ring", timeout=0.1)
        ring = r.json().get("ring", "ring3")
    ring_cache.set(account_id, ring, ttl=60)
    return ring
Blue-Green Deployment: Instant Cutover for Claude API Gateway

Blue-green deployments maintain two fully-provisioned production environments — Blue (current) and Green (next). The load balancer routes 100% of traffic to Blue. When Green is ready and validated, the router flips traffic atomically. Rollback is identical: flip back to Blue. There is no gradual ramp; it is an instantaneous switch.
When to Use Blue-Green vs. Canary/Ringed
Schema changes, breaking API surface changes, DB migrations
Feature flag changes, model upgrades, config tuning
Must segment metrics by version/canary label

For Claude model upgrades, prefer canary/ringed: mixing versions is safe and gradual ramp catches behavioral regressions early. Reserve blue-green for gateway code changes where mixing two versions of the API surface could produce inconsistent client behavior.

# claude-gateway-rollout.yaml
apiVersion: argoproj.io/v1alpha1
kind: Rollout
metadata:
  name: claude-gateway
  namespace: claude-api
spec:
  replicas: 6
  selector:
    matchLabels:
      app: claude-gateway
  template:
    metadata:
      labels:
        app: claude-gateway
    spec:
      containers:
        - name: gateway
          image: claude-gateway:{{ .Values.imageTag }}
          ports:
            - containerPort: 8080
          readinessProbe:
            httpGet:
              path: /healthz
              port: 8080
            initialDelaySeconds: 5
            periodSeconds: 3
  strategy:
    blueGreen:
      activeService: claude-gateway-active    # Blue — receives production traffic
      previewService: claude-gateway-preview   # Green — receives pre-cutover smoke tests
      autoPromotionEnabled: false              # manual promotion gate
      scaleDownDelaySeconds: 600               # keep Blue alive 10 min after promotion
      prePromotionAnalysis:
        templates:
          - templateName: smoke-test
        args:
          - name: service-name
            value: claude-gateway-preview
      postPromotionAnalysis:
        templates:
          - templateName: error-rate-gate
        args:
          - name: service-name
            value: claude-gateway-active

# smoke-test-analysis.yaml
apiVersion: argoproj.io/v1alpha1
kind: AnalysisTemplate
metadata:
  name: smoke-test
  namespace: claude-api
spec:
  args:
    - name: service-name
  metrics:
    - name: smoke-test-suite
      provider:
        job:
          spec:
            template:
              spec:
                containers:
                  - name: smoke-runner
                    image: claude-smoke-tests:latest
                    env:
                      - name: TARGET_URL
                        value: "http://{{args.service-name}}:80"
                      - name: TEST_SUITE
                        value: "smoke"
                    command: ["/bin/sh", "-c", "pytest tests/smoke/ -v --tb=short; exit $?"]
                restartPolicy: Never
            backoffLimit: 0
      successCondition: "result.exitCode == 0"
      failureLimit: 0

# error-rate-analysis.yaml
apiVersion: argoproj.io/v1alpha1
kind: AnalysisTemplate
metadata:
  name: error-rate-gate
  namespace: claude-api
spec:
  args:
    - name: service-name
  metrics:
    - name: error-rate-5xx
      interval: 60s
      count: 10   # observe for 10 minutes post-promotion
      provider:
        prometheus:
          address: http://prometheus:9090
          query: |
            rate(http_requests_total{service="{{args.service-name}}",status=~"5.."}[2m])
            /
            rate(http_requests_total{service="{{args.service-name}}"}[2m])
      successCondition: "result[0] < 0.005"   # < 0.5% 5xx
      failureLimit: 2

Both ringed and blue-green deployments face a common challenge: database schema changes and stateful resources must be compatible with the version being retired, not just the version being promoted. This requires expand/contract (parallel writes) or dual-read patterns.
Expand: add new column / table in a backward-compatible migration. Both Blue and Green can read.
Deploy Green with code that writes to both old and new columns. Run alongside Blue.
Promote Green. Blue is still running (scaleDownDelay). Both write to both columns.
Once Blue is scaled down, contract: remove old column / table in a second migration.

# expand_contract_example.py — dual-write during blue-green overlap period
import os

DEPLOYMENT_VERSION = os.environ.get("DEPLOYMENT_VERSION", "blue")   # "blue" or "green"

def write_request_log(db, request_id: str, model: str, tokens: int):
    """Write to both old and new schema during overlap."""
    # Old schema (Blue reads this)
    db.execute(
        "INSERT INTO request_logs (id, model, tokens) VALUES (?, ?, ?)",
        (request_id, model, tokens)
    )
    # New schema (Green reads this) — only write if column exists
    if DEPLOYMENT_VERSION == "green" or column_exists(db, "request_logs", "tokens_v2"):
        db.execute(
            "INSERT INTO request_logs (id, model, tokens, tokens_v2) VALUES (?, ?, ?, ?)",
            (request_id, model, tokens, tokens * 1.0)   # new field: float instead of int
        )
Use the following decision criteria when designing a release strategy for a Claude API change:
Safe to mix versions; gradual ramp catches quality regressions
Behavioral changes; ring0 dogfood validates correctness first
API gateway code changes (no schema break)
Small blast radius; no environment cost overhead
Breaking API surface change (new required field)
Cannot mix versions; clients must all switch atomically
One infrastructure version must be authoritative at a time
Config-only; ring0 validation sufficient before global push
Combining Strategies in a Single Release

Production Claude releases often compose strategies: a canary starts at 1% within Ring 0, advances to 5% still in Ring 0, then Ring 0 promotes to Ring 1, and so on. A blue-green flip of the gateway layer can happen in parallel for a separate concern (e.g., TLS cert rotation).

# release-pipeline.yaml — GitHub Actions orchestration
name: claude-api-release
on:
  workflow_dispatch:
    inputs:
      version:
        description: "New model/gateway version tag"

jobs:
  ring0-canary:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - name: Deploy canary to ring0 (1%)
        run: ./scripts/canary_deploy.sh ring0 ${{ github.event.inputs.version }} 1
      - name: Soak 24h, evaluate gate
        run: sleep 86400 && ./scripts/advance_ring.py ring0 ${{ github.event.inputs.version }}

  ring1-promotion:
    needs: ring0-canary
    steps:
      - name: Advance ring0 → ring1 (5%)
        run: ./scripts/advance_ring.py ring0 ${{ github.event.inputs.version }}

  ring2-promotion:
    needs: ring1-promotion
    steps:
      - name: Advance ring1 → ring2 (20%)
        run: ./scripts/advance_ring.py ring1 ${{ github.event.inputs.version }}

  ring3-ga:
    needs: ring2-promotion
    steps:
      - name: Advance ring2 → ring3 (100% GA)
        run: ./scripts/advance_ring.py ring2 ${{ github.event.inputs.version }}
Ring config reviewed and account assignments verified against customer tier data
Green environment smoke-tested against preview endpoint before LB flip
DB expand migration applied and verified on both Blue and Green
On-call briefed: ring schedule, rollback commands, escalation path
PagerDuty silence windows set for expected metric shifts during ring transitions
Soak duration timers started (in Argo Rollouts or external workflow orchestrator)
Hold at current ring, open incident
Stop ring advancement; route ring back to previous version
Abort before promote if caught pre-promotion
Rollback DB migration first, then rollback deployment
Rollback DB migration, then abort rollout

# Ring-level error rate
rate(claude_api_errors_total{ring="ring1",version="v2"}[5m])
/ rate(claude_api_requests_total{ring="ring1",version="v2"}[5m])

# Blue-Green post-promotion latency comparison
histogram_quantile(0.99,
  rate(http_request_duration_seconds_bucket{app="claude-gateway",env="green"}[5m])
)

# Ring advancement eligibility (sample count check)
increase(claude_api_requests_total{ring="ring1",version="v2"}[48h])

Ringed deployments and blue-green deployments give you two orthogonal axes of safety for Claude API releases. Ringed deployments control who gets the change first, soak at each audience boundary, and require explicit gate passage before widening. Blue-green deployments control the cutover speed and rollback latency — an instantaneous flip with a sub-30-second undo. Use ringed deployments for behavioral changes (model upgrades, prompt changes) and blue-green for API surface or infrastructure changes where version mixing is unsafe. Compose them within a single release pipeline to achieve both gradual audience validation and instant rollback capability.
Ring 0 (internal) → Ring 1 (beta) → Ring 2 (early adopters) → Ring 3 (GA), with soak gates at each boundary
Blue-Green maintains two full environments; cutover is a load-balancer flip
DB migrations use expand/contract to survive the overlap period when both environments are live
Argo Rollouts implements both strategies natively; combine with GitHub Actions for multi-ring pipeline orchestration
Rollback in ringed deployments reverts the ring config; rollback in blue-green aborts the Argo Rollout
